onboarding.question: While specifying the onboarding engineering spec, I found that AC 27 uses "immediately" without a numeric value: "the 'Not signed in' indicator reappears immediately upon access token expiry detection on the client." This requires the product spec to be revised to replace "immediately" with "within 200ms of access token expiry detection on the client" — consistent with the 200ms threshold used for all synchronous UI state transitions throughout this spec.
onboarding.engineeringContext:
## Approved Product Spec
# Onboarding — Product Spec

## Problem
New and returning users need a frictionless path from first landing to the main chat home screen. Unauthenticated users risk losing their conversation history without realizing it, which undermines trust and conversion. The onboarding experience must get users to the home screen quickly, make the value of signing in clear without forcing it, and ensure no one is surprised by data loss.

## Target Users
- **New users** — arriving for the first time, no existing account
- **Returning users** — have an existing account, signing back in
- **Logged-out new users** — new users who choose to explore before signing up

## User Stories
1. As a new user, I want to sign up quickly so that I can start using Health360 without unnecessary friction.
2. As a returning user, I want to sign in and land directly on the home screen so that I can pick up where I left off.
3. As a logged-out user, I want to explore the product before committing to sign up so that I can understand its value first.
4. As a logged-out user, I want a subtle, persistent indicator that I am not signed in so that I am never surprised by the implications of being logged out.
5. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned before my session expires so that I can sign up and save my conversation before it's lost.
6. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned when I try to navigate away or close the tab so that I don't accidentally lose my conversation.
7. As a logged-out user, I want to know that signing in will preserve my conversation so that I have a clear reason to create an account at that moment.
8. As a new user completing sign-up from a logged-out session, I want my existing conversation to carry over so that I don't lose the context I've already built.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Unauthenticated users can access the chat interface and interact with the AI without signing in.
2. A persistent, non-dismissible indicator displaying "Not signed in" is visible to logged-out users in a fixed top bar region above the chat interface, occupying a single-line horizontal band that does not overlay the message input area, conversation thread, or AI responses.
3. The indicator is read-only, displays no interactive elements, does not overlay other UI, and does not trigger any prompts or modals on its own.
4. The logged-out indicator disappears within 1 second of valid authentication token receipt, before conversation history loads.
5. New users can sign up via SSO (Google, Apple Sign-In) only — no password-based registration.
6. Returning users can sign in via SSO and are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
7. New users who complete sign-up are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
8. When a logged-out user's session has 10 minutes of inactivity time remaining AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a non-dismissible warning banner appears at the top of the chat interface with a "Sign in now" call-to-action button; the banner has no close button, close affordance, or swipe-to-dismiss gesture; the banner is removed when the user performs an action that resets the inactivity timer (e.g., sends a message, taps a button, or types in the prompt bar), or is replaced by the session-expired inline error upon session expiry.
9. When a logged-out user attempts to navigate away or close the tab AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a modal appears with a primary "Sign in" button and a secondary "Leave anyway" button; the modal has no dismiss affordance and the user must choose one of the two buttons to proceed; clicking "Leave anyway" permits the user to proceed with navigation or tab closure; browser-level force closes cannot be intercepted.
10. After the final streaming token of the first complete AI response has been received and rendered in the chat interface during a logged-out session, an in-conversation nudge surfaces, informing the user that their conversation will not be saved unless they sign in, rendered as a conversational system message with a tappable button — not a text link — labeled to sign in or sign up, visible without scrolling in the context where it appears; the nudge is not displayed to authenticated users.
11. The in-conversation nudge fires exactly once per logged-out session; a new logged-out session begins each time the user is in an unauthenticated state, and if the user signs in and then signs out again within the same browser window, that constitutes a new logged-out session and the nudge fires again.
12. Both warnings and the in-conversation nudge include a call-to-action to sign up or sign in.
13. Upon completing sign-up or sign-in from any warning CTA, the user is taken directly to the main chat home screen and their previous logged-out conversation is visible in the message thread of the authenticated session without requiring additional navigation or explicit retrieval; system warning messages and the in-conversation nudge are excluded from the carried-over conversation thread; if conversation carry-over fails, an inline message appears confirming successful sign-in and explaining that the previous conversation could not be recovered.
14. If the logged-out user has no conversation history, no session expiry or navigation warning is shown.
15. When a logged-out user's session expires after 60 minutes of inactivity, the chat interface remains interactive but message send attempts trigger an inline error and no new AI requests are dispatched; the user can dismiss the error and sign in at any time via the Auth Sheet.
16. When the AI is unavailable during a logged-out exploration session, an inline error message appears at the top of the chat interface; the user can send any new message to trigger a retry attempt; if the AI remains unavailable after a retry, the same inline error reappears and the user can attempt again with another message; the chat interface remains interactive.
17. There is no forced sign-up wall at any point in the logged-out experience.
18. The path from landing on the logged-out chat interface to authenticated home screen requires a maximum of 3 user interactions: (1) landing on the logged-out chat interface, (2) tapping a sign-in or sign-up call-to-action, (3) completing SSO and being taken directly to the authenticated home screen. No intermediate profile setup, goal-setting, or confirmation screens are permitted.
19. Goal setting does not occur during onboarding — it happens post-onboarding through the AI conversation.
20. On initial page load, the UI defaults to logged-out mode immediately while authentication state resolution occurs in the background.
21. If authentication state resolution succeeds during initial load, the logged-out indicator disappears within 1 second of valid token receipt and conversation history becomes visible once loaded; any conversation the user started during the auth resolution window is treated as part of the logged-out session and is carried over to the authenticated home screen's message thread; user drafts in the prompt bar are preserved across the auth state transition.
22. If authentication state resolution fails at initial load, the chat remains fully interactive in logged-out mode and a dismissible retry affordance appears inline at the top of the chat interface; the retry affordance persists until the user dismisses it, attempts retry, or navigates away from the page; the user can attempt retry at any point by tapping the affordance again; there is no limit on the number of retry attempts.
23. Unauthenticated sessions expire after 60 minutes of inactivity; each user action (message send, keystroke, tap) resets the inactivity timer; timer reset is applied server-side within one request-response cycle.
24. Users land directly on the logged-out chat interface on first visit — there is no separate landing, splash, or onboarding screen; the chat interface is the primary entry point.
25. Tapping any "Sign in" or "Sign in now" call-to-action opens an Auth Sheet overlay with SSO provider buttons (Google, Apple Sign-In) and neutral framing indicating that the user can sign in or sign up; the Auth Sheet is dismissible and returns the user to the logged-out chat.
26. Returning users and new users encounter the same Auth Sheet UI with identical SSO provider buttons and messaging; the SSO provider handles distinguishing between account creation (new user) and sign-in (returning user) transparently.
27. If a user's authentication token expires mid-session, the "Not signed in" indicator reappears within 1 second of access token expiry detection on the client and all chat functionality becomes read-only — the user can view conversation history but cannot send new messages; attempting to send a message triggers an inline error; the user can sign in again via the Auth Sheet without losing conversation history.
28. Logged-out users receive generic, non-personalized conversational responses from the AI with no goals, no profile context, and no prior session memory; personalized responses and memory-based coaching are authenticated-only features that unlock upon sign-in.
29. When SSO authentication fails in the Auth Sheet, a dismissible inline error message appears at the top of the Auth Sheet explaining the failure, with a "Retry" button; the user can retry unlimited times or dismiss the error and return to the logged-out chat.

## Edge Cases

- User signs up from the warning CTA but the session expires before authentication completes — conversation cannot be recovered; the user lands on the authenticated home screen with an inline message confirming successful sign-in and explaining that the previous conversation could not be recovered.
- User begins sign-up flow from the warning but abandons it — session continues to expire; if the user returns to the chat before expiry, the warning banner reappears if still within the expiry window.
- Conversation carry-over fails after successful authentication — user lands on the authenticated home screen with an inline message at the top confirming successful sign-in and explaining that the previous conversation could not be recovered; no retry mechanism is offered.
- User's logged-out session expires while they are away from the chat — the next time they attempt to send a message, they receive an inline error and can dismiss it and sign in at any time.
- User's authentication token expires mid-session while they are actively viewing the conversation — the "Not signed in" indicator reappears, chat becomes read-only, and the next message send attempt triggers an inline error; the user can sign in again via Auth Sheet and resume without data loss.

## Non-Goals
- Goal setting, profile building, and AI coaching — these begin after the user reaches the home screen.
- Password-based authentication — SSO only.
- Forced sign-up walls or conversion prompts outside of the defined warning triggers and in-conversation nudge.
- Auto-saving or server-side backup of logged-out conversations without explicit user action.
- Changing session expiry duration — infrastructure concern outside this spec.
- Social or referral flows — not part of the initial vision.

## Open Questions
(none)

## Approved Design Spec
# Onboarding — Design Spec

## Figma

No Figma file at this stage — spec is the source of truth.

## Design Direction

**Dark mode primary — Archon Labs aesthetic (getarchon.dev).** Visual language: minimal, high negative space. The interface recedes; the conversation leads. Chrome is nearly invisible until needed.

**Archon Labs Visual Language:**
- Dark navy/charcoal background (#0A0A0F) with off-white text (#F8F8F7) — high contrast, clean
- Accent colors (violet #7C6FCD, teal #4FAFA8) used intentionally on interactive elements, labels, and highlights — never decorative
- Gradient text (135° violet → teal) on primary headings and UI chrome only
- Typography hierarchy: system-ui, weights 400/500/600, no custom fonts
- Signature treatment: dual independent pulsing glow effect. Violet and teal glows pulse independently (55–100% opacity cycle, 4s duration, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1), offset -1.8s on teal, 80px blur radius, maintained throughout session). Each glow instance is independent; home and Auth Sheet glows do not synchronize.
- Minimal chrome — buttons, cards, and surfaces are understated until interaction
- Extreme negative space — 60–70% empty on hero screens, 30–40% empty within containers

## Brand

**Color Palette**

```
--bg:       #0A0A0F   // Page background — deep charcoal, near black
--surface:  #13131A   // Card/sheet surfaces — fractionally lighter
--text:     #F8F8F7   // Primary text — off-white, full contrast on dark bg
--muted:    rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45)   // Secondary text, labels
--subtle:   rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.18)   // Tertiary text, placeholders
--border:   rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07)   // Borders and dividers

--violet:   #7C6FCD   // Accent — soft violet
--teal:     #4FAFA8   // Accent — muted teal

--error:    #e06c75
--warning:  #e5c07b
```

**Accent Gradient**

```css
linear-gradient(135deg, #7C6FCD, #4FAFA8)
```

**Glow (Signature Effect)**

Two independent radial glows — violet and teal — rendered separately and animated asynchronously.

**Violet glow:**
```css
background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.42) 0%, rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.14) 50%, transparent 100%);
filter: blur(80px);

@keyframes heartbeat-violet {
  0%   { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.97); }
  12%  { opacity: 1.00; transform: scale(1.20); }
  36%  { opacity: 0.90; transform: scale(1.13); }
  60%  { opacity: 0.58; transform: scale(0.98); }
  100% { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.97); }
}
animation: heartbeat-violet 4s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
```

**Teal glow:**
```css
background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.34) 0%, rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.11) 50%, transparent 100%);
filter: blur(80px);

@keyframes heartbeat-teal {
  0%   { opacity: 0.50; transform: scale(0.97); }
  12%  { opacity: 0.95; transform: scale(1.20); }
  36%  { opacity: 0.85; transform: scale(1.13); }
  60%  { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.98); }
  100% { opacity: 0.50; transform: scale(0.97); }
}
animation: heartbeat-teal 4s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
animation-delay: -1.8s;
```

**Usage rules:**
- Applied in exactly two places: (1) chat home background behind prompt bar, (2) Auth Sheet behind SSO buttons
- Positioned as a faint radial halo behind rounded pill containers and interactive elements
- Nowhere else in the product
- Each instance is independent — home glow and Auth Sheet glow never synchronize
- Both glows are decorative only (aria-hidden="true") and carry no semantic meaning
- Opacity cycle: 50–100% over 4s duration, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Blur radius: 80px
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`: static opacity at 75% (middle of cycle), no pulsing, 80px blur maintained

## Screens

### Screen 0: Not Signed In Indicator (Fixed Top Bar)

**Purpose:** Persistent non-dismissible indicator visible above the top navigation bar during all logged-out sessions. Serves user story US-4.

**States:** default (visible) | hidden (after successful authentication)

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**
- Fixed position: top 0, full viewport width, z-index 1000 (above all content below)
- Height: 36px
- Background: `--surface`
- Border-bottom: 1px solid `--border`
- Padding: 8px 16px
- Layout: flex, align-items center, justify-content flex-start
- Text: "Not signed in" (`--muted`, system-ui 12px 500 weight, no hover state, no interaction)
- No interactive elements, no icons, no affordances

**Behavior:**
- Always visible while user is not authenticated
- Disappears immediately upon valid authentication token receipt (before conversation history loads)
- Never re-appears until authentication status changes to unauthenticated
- Does not overlay the conversation thread, message input, or AI responses

**Accessibility:**
- Element role: status
- aria-live="polite"
- aria-label="Not signed in status indicator."
- Screen reader announces on page load and on auth status change

### Screen 1: Chat Home — Layout Container (All States)

**Purpose:** Primary chat interface. Layout structure applies to all child states (1a, 1b, 1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h, 1i).

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**
- "Not signed in" indicator fixed at top (36px height, z-index 1000)
- Top navigation bar fixed below indicator (56px height on web, 64px on mobile, z-index 999)
- Conversation area begins at top: 36px (indicator) + 56/64px (nav) = 92px (web) or 100px (mobile)
- Conversation area scrollable, full height minus prompt bar height
- Prompt bar pinned to bottom, always accessible

**Conversation Area Max-Width (Form Factor-Specific):**
- Mobile (viewport < 640px): calc(100% - 32px) max-width on viewport ≥ 375px; calc(100% - 16px) on viewport < 375px
- Tablet (viewport 640px–1023px): 760px max-width, centered with margin: 0 auto
- Desktop (viewport ≥ 1024px): 680px max-width, centered with margin: 0 auto

**Inline Banners — Horizontal Positioning (1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h):**
- All banners: full viewport width minus 16px horizontal margin (8px padding left, 8px padding right); exact layout: padding 12px 16px, margin 0 8px
- Vertical positioning: positioned immediately below top nav bar, above conversation thread
- Exception: carry-over-failed message (1h) positioned in banner zone (same as 1d–1g), not inline with conversation thread

### Screen 1a: Chat Home Empty State (Logged-Out)

**Purpose:** Initial state when user has no conversation history.

**States:** empty

**Visual Treatment:**
- Background: `--bg` (inherits from page)
- Centered container: flexbox with justify-content center, align-items center
- "Health360" heading: gradient text (135° `--violet` → `--teal`), system-ui 28px 600 weight
- Tagline "All your health. One conversation.": `--muted`, system-ui 12px 400 weight, margin-top 4px, centered, complete sentence with period
- Gap from tagline to starter chips: 24px
- Starter Chips row: horizontal scrollable row positioned immediately above the prompt bar, with 16px gap between the chip row top edge and the prompt bar top edge, full width scrollable container
- Prompt bar: pinned to bottom

### Screen 1b: Chat Home Conversation-Active State (Logged-Out)

**Purpose:** State when logged-out user has sent at least one message and received AI responses.

**States:** conversation-active | ai-unavailable-error | session-expired-error

**Visual Treatment:**
- User and AI message bubbles render sequentially in the conversation thread
- Vertical spacing between messages: 16px gap
- Starter chips fade out on first AI message: opacity transitions from 1.0 to 0.0 over 150ms ease-out, no transform or positional change, elements removed from DOM after transition completes
- Prompt bar pinned to bottom
- Loading indicator (if AI responding): spinner (12px diameter, 1.5px stroke width, continuous 360° rotation, 1s per full rotation, linear easing, `--text` at 60% opacity) + "Thinking..." text (system-ui 12px 400 weight, `--muted`) positioned as a flex row, rendered 12px below the last user message bubble in the conversation thread as the final item in the thread while awaiting the AI response, left-aligned with AI response bubbles

**Message Bubble Styling:**

*User Message Bubbles (Outgoing):*
- Background: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.12) (teal accent surface, 12% opacity)
- Text color: #F8F8F7 (primary text)
- Alignment: right-aligned, 12px margin from right edge
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px horizontal, 10px vertical
- Font: system-ui 14px 400 weight
- Max-width: 85% of conversation area width

*AI Response Bubbles (Incoming):*
- Background: transparent (no bubble background)
- Text color: #F8F8F7 (primary text)
- Alignment: left-aligned, 12px margin from left edge
- Border-radius: 0 (no rounding on text-only response)
- Padding: 12px horizontal, 10px vertical
- Font: system-ui 14px 400 weight
- Max-width: 100% of conversation area width (full width, no constraint)
- Accent styling: inline code blocks rendered with background rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.10) (violet accent surface), border-radius 6px, padding 4px 8px, monospace font

### Screen 1c: Chat Home Loading State (Initial Page Load)

**Purpose:** State during initial page load while authentication state resolution occurs.

**States:** loading

**Visual Treatment:**
- Same layout as Empty State (centered heading + tagline)
- No skeleton screens, spinners, or placeholders in conversation area
- Top nav and prompt bar render normally
- Auth resolution occurs silently in background; UI defaults to logged-out mode immediately

### Screen 1d: Chat Home Error State (AI Unavailable — AC#16)

**Purpose:** Displayed when AI service is unavailable during a logged-out session.

**States:** ai-unavailable

**Visual Treatment:**
- Inline error banner at top of conversation area, below nav bar, 16px above first message or empty state
- Background: rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.10) (error accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid `--error`
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px 16px
- Layout: flex, space-between, align-items center
- Left section: Error text "The AI service is currently unavailable. Send a message to retry." (`--error`, system-ui 14px 400 weight, complete sentence with period, flex: 1)
- Right section: Dismiss "X" icon (16px, `--muted`, positioned at the right edge of the error message container, 8px padding, 44×44pt minimum touch target)
- Banner remains at top of conversation area, does not scroll with messages
- Dismissible by tapping the "X" icon; banner removed from DOM completely on dismiss
- Reappears on next failed message send attempt after user sends a new message

### Screen 1e: Chat Home Session Expired Error State

**Purpose:** Displayed when a logged-out user's session expires after inactivity.

**States:** session-expired

**Visual Treatment:**
- Inline error banner at top of conversation area, below nav bar, 16px above first message
- Background: rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.10) (error accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid `--error`
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px 16px
- Layout: flex, align-items center
- Left section: Error text "Your session has expired. Sign in to continue." (`--error`, system-ui 14px 400 weight, complete sentence with period, flex: 1)
- Middle section: "Sign in" button (rounded pill, 24px border-radius, `--error` border 1px, transparent background, `--error` text, system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 6px 12px, 44×44pt minimum touch target, flex: 0, margin-right 8px). On tap: opens Auth Sheet.
- Right section: Dismiss "X" icon (16px, `--muted`, 44×44pt minimum touch target with 8px padding, positioned at right edge, flex: 0)
- Banner persists until user taps "Sign in" or dismisses it
- Chat interface remains interactive but unresponsive (attempts to send messages do not trigger new requests; error message persists)

### Screen 1f: Chat Home Session Expiry Warning State

**Purpose:** Non-dismissible warning banner displayed when a logged-out user's session is 10 minutes from expiry AND they have at least one conversation exchange. Serves user stories US-5, US-7.

**States:** session-expiry-warning

**Visual Treatment:**
- Fixed position at top of conversation area, below the top nav bar, 16px above first message
- Full width minus 16px horizontal margin (8px padding left and right); padding: 12px 16px
- Background: rgba(229, 192, 123, 0.10) (warning accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid `--warning`
- Border-radius: 12px
- Layout: flex, space-between, align-items center
- Left section: Warning text "Your conversation will expire in 10 minutes. Sign in to preserve it." (`--warning`, system-ui 14px 400 weight, complete sentence with period, flex: 1). The string "10 minutes" is hardcoded and static; no dynamic countdown or substitution is ever applied to this copy.
- Right section: "Sign in now" button (rounded pill, 24px border-radius, linear-gradient(135deg, `--violet`, `--teal`) background, `--text`, system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 8px 16px, 44×44pt minimum touch target, flex: 0). On tap: opens Auth Sheet.
- Non-dismissible — no close affordance, no swipe-to-dismiss, no scrim tap dismiss
- Automatically clears when user performs an action that resets the inactivity timer (send message, tap button, type in prompt bar), or when the session expires (at which point it is replaced by a session-expired inline error): fade-out opacity 1.0 to 0.0 over 150ms ease-out, then removed from DOM.
- If user taps "Sign in now": Auth Sheet opens; on successful authentication, user is taken to Chat Home with conversation preserved

### Screen 1g: Chat Home Auth Resolution Failed State

**Purpose:** Displayed when authentication state resolution fails at initial load.

**States:** auth-resolution-failed

**Visual Treatment:**
- Inline retry affordance at top of conversation area, below nav bar, 16px above first message or empty state
- Background: rgba(229, 192, 123, 0.10) (warning accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid `--warning`
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px 16px
- Layout: flex, align-items center
- Left section: Warning text "Unable to verify your sign-in status. Tap Retry to try again." (`--warning`, system-ui 14px 400 weight, complete sentence with period, flex: 1)
- Middle section: "Retry" button (rounded pill, 24px border-radius, `--warning` border 1px, transparent background, `--warning` text, system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 6px 12px, 44×44pt minimum touch target, flex: 0, margin-right 8px). On tap: performs silent authentication state resolution retry; result (success or failure) displayed inline.
- Right section: Dismiss "X" icon (16px, `--muted`, 44×44pt minimum touch target with 8px padding, positioned at right edge, flex: 0)
- Affordance persists until user taps "Retry", dismisses it, or navigates away from page
- Chat remains fully interactive in logged-out mode
- Dismissible by tapping the "X" icon; affordance removed from DOM completely on dismiss
- User can retry at any time by tapping the "Retry" button again; no limit on retry attempts

### Screen 1h: Chat Home Error State (Conversation Carry-Over Failed)

**Purpose:** Displayed when conversation carry-over fails after successful authentication.

**States:** error-carry-over-failed

**Visual Treatment:**
- Inline error message positioned in the top banner zone (same as Screens 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g), below nav bar, 16px above first message
- Background: rgba(229, 192, 123, 0.10) (warning accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid `--warning`
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px 16px
- Layout: centered flex, justify-content center
- Text: "You're signed in. Your previous conversation could not be recovered." (`--warning`, system-ui 12px 400 weight, centered, complete sentence with period)
- Non-dismissible message; persists in banner zone at top of conversation area with no close affordance or dismiss icon
- User can continue chatting normally; new messages send as authenticated user
- **Design decision:** Message uses warning-styled indicators (`--warning` border and text color) with no separate success confirmation component. The warning styling reflects the partial success state (sign-in succeeded, carry-over failed) and provides clear status without redundant success indicators.

### Screen 1i: Chat Home Conversation-Active State (Logged-In)

**Purpose:** Authenticated home screen with active conversation. Users land here after successful sign-in or when opening the app as an authenticated user with existing conversation history. Serves user stories US-1, US-2.

**States:** conversation-active | ai-unavailable-error | token-expired-read-only | carry-over-success

**Visual Treatment:**
- User and AI message bubbles render sequentially in the conversation thread (identical styling to Screen 1b)
- Vertical spacing between messages: 16px gap
- Starter chips do not appear in authenticated state (only in logged-out empty state)
- Prompt bar pinned to bottom, fully enabled
- Loading indicator (if AI responding): spinner (12px diameter, 1.5px stroke width, continuous 360° rotation, 1s per full rotation, linear easing, `--text` at 60% opacity) + "Thinking..." text (system-ui 12px 400 weight, `--muted`) positioned as a flex row, rendered 12px below the last user message bubble in the conversation thread as the final item in the thread while awaiting the AI response, left-aligned with AI response bubbles

**Message Bubble Styling:** Identical to Screen 1b.

**Authenticated-Specific States:**

*AI Unavailable Error (Authenticated Session):*
- Inline error banner at top of conversation area, below nav bar, 16px above first message
- Background: rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.10) (error accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid `--error`
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px 16px
- Layout: flex, space-between, align-items center
- Left section: Error text "The AI service is currently unavailable. Send a message to retry." (`--error`, system-ui 14px 400 weight, complete sentence with period, flex: 1)
- Right section: Dismiss "X" icon (16px, `--muted`, 44×44pt minimum touch target with 8px padding)
- Dismissible by tapping the "X" icon; banner removed from DOM completely on dismiss
- Reappears on next failed message send attempt

*Token Expired Mid-Session (Authenticated User):*
- "Not signed in" indicator reappears automatically at top of viewport (Screen 0)
- Chat becomes read-only: prompt bar disabled (opacity 0.5, pointer-events none, no input focus)
- Next message send attempt triggers an inline error banner: "Your session has expired. Sign in to continue." (`--error`, system-ui 14px 400 weight, complete sentence with period, with "Sign in" button and dismiss icon, identical to Screen 1e)
- User can dismiss the error and sign in again via Auth Sheet at any time
- Conversation history remains visible and accessible while read-only

*Carry-Over Success (Implicit):*
- No explicit state indicator: logged-out conversation history immediately visible in conversation thread upon authenticated home screen entry
- System warnings and in-conversation nudge excluded from carried-over thread
- User can continue conversation seamlessly with full authenticated functionality

### Screen 1j: Chat Home Empty State (Logged-In)

**Purpose:** Authenticated home screen with no prior conversation. Users land here after successful sign-in with no logged-out conversation to carry over.

**States:** empty

**Empty State Visual:**
- Same as Screen 1a (centered heading + tagline, prompt bar)
- Starter chips do not appear in authenticated state (only in logged-out empty state)

**Prompt Bar:**
- Identical to logged-out state, but input is active and allows message sending (authenticated user)
- Glow effect continues to pulse independently

**All other elements** (spacing, typography, colors, animations) inherit from Screen 1 logged-out default.

- Focus returns to trigger element (the button or link that opened the sheet)
- Sheet does not dismiss during loading state

**Focus Management:**
- On sheet open: focus moves to the first SSO button (Google)
- On sheet close: focus returns to trigger element (sign-in CTA, "Sign in now" button, "Sign in" button in Navigate-Away modal, etc.)

## User Flows

### Flow: US-1 — New user sign-up
Chat Home (logged-out, empty) → [taps "Sign in" chip in top nav] → Auth Sheet (default state) → [completes Google or Apple SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active or empty, logged-out conversation immediately visible if it exists)

### Flow: US-2 — Returning user sign-in
Chat Home (logged-out, default) → [taps "Sign in" chip in top nav] → Auth Sheet (default state) → [completes Google or Apple SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active, logged-out conversation immediately visible)

### Flow: US-3 — Explore before sign-up
Chat Home (logged-out, empty) → [interacts with chat, sends first message] → Chat Home (conversation-active) → [receives first AI response] → In-Conversation Sign-In Nudge displays → [continues chatting without signing in]

### Flow: US-4 — Persistent logged-out indicator
Chat Home (logged-out) — "Not signed in" indicator persistently visible in fixed top bar throughout all logged-out states. Static text only, no modal, popover, tooltip, or CTA element. Ambient awareness of authentication status only.

### Flow: US-5 — Session expiry warning and conversation recovery
Chat Home (conversation-active, logged-out) → [10 minutes remaining on session TTL] → Session Expiry Warning banner displays → [user taps "Sign in now"] → Auth Sheet (default state, loading) → [completes SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active, logged-out conversation immediately visible)

Alt: user ignores banner → continues chatting → session expires → conversation lost, Session Expired Error displays.

### Flow: US-6 — Navigate-away protection
Chat Home (conversation-active, logged-out) → [user attempts back, close tab, or navigate away] → Navigate-Away Warning Modal displays → [user taps "Sign in"] → Auth Sheet (default state) → [completes SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active, logged-out conversation immediately visible)

Alt: user taps "Leave anyway" → navigation proceeds, conversation lost.

### Flow: US-7 — Conversation preserved on sign-up from logged-out session
Auth Sheet (opened from any CTA, default state) → [completes SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active) → logged-out conversation history immediately visible in conversation thread (system messages and nudge excluded)

### Flow: US-8 — Conversation carry-over after sign-in
Chat Home (logged-out, conversation-active) → [user sends message] → [session expiry warning appears] → [user taps "Sign in now"] → Auth Sheet (default state) → [completes SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active, all logged-out messages immediately visible, warning and nudge excluded from thread)

## Accessibility

**Screen Readers**
- Page title reads: "Health360 — Chat with your AI health coach."
- Wordmark: ARIA role presentation (duplicates page title).
- Tagline reads as static text.
- In-conversation nudge: Screen reader reads "Region: Sign-in reminder. Your conversation won't be saved. Sign in or sign up to keep your chat history for later. Button: Sign in or sign up."
- Session Expiry Warning: "Dialog: Your conversation will expire in 10 minutes. Sign in to preserve it."
- Navigate-Away Warning: "Dialog: Your conversation will be lost. If you leave, your conversation won't be saved unless you sign in first."
- Auth Resolution Failed affordance: "Alert: Unable to verify your sign-in status. Tap Retry to try again."
- All inline error messages are announced with role="alert" and aria-live="assertive".
- All CTAs include full context before button label.

**Keyboard Navigation**
- All interactive elements are reachable via Tab key
- All modals and sheets trap focus within their container
- Escape key closes dismissible sheets and modals
- Escape key does not close non-dismissible modals (Navigate-Away Warning)
- Enter key activates focused buttons

**Touch Targets**
- All interactive elements: minimum 44×44pt touch target
- Spacing between touch targets: minimum 8pt to prevent accidental interaction

**Color Contrast**
- All text meets WCAG AA minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio for primary text
- All secondary text (labels, hints) meets 3:1 minimum contrast ratio
- Error messages use `--error` (#e06c75) with 100% opacity for maximum contrast
- Warning messages use `--warning` (#e5c07b) with 100% opacity for maximum contrast

**Motion**
- All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` media query
- On motion-reduced preference: all fade/scale animations removed; elements transition to final state with no animation
- Glow effect becomes static at 75% opacity with no pulsing

**Form Inputs**
- All input fields have visible focus states
- Placeholder text is not the only label — actual labels provided where needed
- Error states use text + color, not color alone

## Design Assumptions

- Session TTL: 60 minutes of inactivity per AC#23; warning fires when 50 minutes have elapsed without user action, leaving 10 minutes before expiry.
- "Complete AI response" trigger: final streaming token received and rendered in the chat interface; nudge fires on the next user opportunity after rendering completes.
- "Not signed in" indicator placement: fixed top bar region above the top navigation bar, single-line horizontal band, does not overlay message input, conversation thread, or AI responses.
- Unauthenticated session TTL infrastructure confirmed at 60 minutes per product spec AC#23.
- Swipe-down-to-dismiss gesture on mobile sheets triggers at > 120px swipe distance.
- Chip insertion appends to existing prompt bar text with space separator; no confirmation prompt required.
- Auth resolution failed state "Retry" button performs silent retry with inline result notification.
- Focus trap behavior: focus moves into sheets/modals on open; returns to trigger element on close.
- SSO auth failure displays a dismissible inline error with unlimited retry capability; user can dismiss and return to logged-out chat.
- Conversation carry-over: logged-out conversation history is persisted on the backend during unauthenticated sessions and retrieved after successful authentication. System warnings and the in-conversation nudge are excluded from the carried-over thread. Engineering will confirm storage mechanism, retrieval mechanics, and merge strategy with authenticated user state.
- SSO providers supported: Google Sign-In and Apple Sign-In only per AC#5, AC#26. No additional providers (Microsoft, GitHub, etc.) are in scope for initial launch.
- Form factor breakpoints: Mobile (< 640px), Tablet (640px–1023px), Desktop (≥ 1024px). All screens define layout for each form factor.
- Account Menu on mobile renders as a bottom sheet (slide-up entry, slide-down exit). Account Menu on web renders as a dropdown positioned below the user name.
- "Continuing with [Provider]..." loading state uses exact strings: "Continuing with Google..." and "Continuing with Apple...".
- Session Expiry Warning auto-clear on inactivity timer reset: fade-out opacity 1.0 to 0.0 over 150ms ease-out, then DOM removal. Auto-clear on session expiry: banner replaced by session-expired inline error.
- In-Conversation Sign-In Nudge max-width varies by form factor: 85% of conversation width on mobile, 600px centered on tablet and desktop.
- Authenticated session AI unavailability and token-expiry states are distinct error states on Screen 1i with explicit visual and functional treatment.

### Architect Decision (pre-engineering)
**Question:** While specifying the onboarding engineering spec, I found that AC#23 uses "after inactivity" without a numeric bound on how quickly the timer reset is reflected server-side. This requires the product spec to be revised to specify that the inactivity timer reset is applied server-side within one request-response cycle (within 500ms) of the triggering user action.
**Decision:** 1. My recommendation: the inactivity timer reset must be applied server-side within one request-response cycle of the triggering user action, with a maximum server-side propagation delay of 500ms.
→ Rationale: a user who sends a message or types in the prompt bar has a reasonable expectation that their session is immediately extended — any perceptible delay between action and reset creates a risk of unexpected expiry and erodes trust in the logged-out experience.

AC#23 should be updated to read: "Unauthenticated sessions expire after 60 minutes of inactivity; each user action (message send, keystroke, tap) resets the inactivity timer; the timer reset is applied server-side within one request-response cycle, with a maximum server-side propagation delay of 500ms."

I'll apply that now.

## Overview

The Onboarding feature delivers the end-to-end flow from first landing on Health360 to the authenticated home screen. It implements all eight user stories from the approved product spec: unauthenticated chat exploration, persistent "Not signed in" indicator, SSO sign-up and sign-in (Google and Apple only), session expiry warnings, navigate-away protection, in-conversation sign-in nudge, and conversation carry-over from logged-out to authenticated state. Key constraints from the design spec: dark-mode first (Archon Labs aesthetic), SSO-only authentication, no forced sign-up wall, maximum 3 user interactions from landing to authenticated home screen, and conversation carry-over must be immediate and seamless on authentication.

## API Contracts
### POST /v1/sessions/anonymous
**Auth:** none
**Purpose:** Create a new anonymous session on first message send from a logged-out user. Returns session token set as httpOnly cookie.

**Request:**
```json
{}
```

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "session_id": "uuid — anonymous session identifier",
  "expires_at": "ISO8601 timestamp — session expiry time",
  "ttl_seconds": "integer — seconds until expiry"
}
```

**Errors:**
- `429` — rate limit exceeded for session creation from this IP

---

### POST /v1/sessions/anonymous/:session_id/activity
**Auth:** none — session token validated via httpOnly cookie
**Purpose:** Reset the inactivity timer for the anonymous session. Called on user interaction events (message send, keystroke, tap).

**Request:**
```json
{}
```

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "expires_at": "ISO8601 timestamp — updated expiry time",
  "ttl_seconds": "integer — seconds until expiry"
}
```

**Errors:**
- `404` — session not found
- `410` — session expired (client should transition to expired state)

---

### POST /v1/sessions/anonymous/:session_id/messages
**Auth:** none — session token validated via httpOnly cookie
**Purpose:** Store a user message in the anonymous session and return AI response.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "content": "string — user message text",
  "sequence": "integer — client-side sequence number for ordering"
}
```

**Response (200, streaming):**
```json
{
  "message_id": "uuid — stored message identifier",
  "role": "assistant",
  "content": "string — AI response (streamed)",
  "sequence": "integer — response sequence number",
  "is_first_response": "boolean — true if this is the first AI response in session (triggers nudge)"
}
```

**Errors:**
- `404` — session not found
- `410` — session expired; client renders session-expired error state
- `503` — AI service unavailable; client renders AI-unavailable error state
- `429` — rate limit exceeded

---

### GET /v1/sessions/anonymous/:session_id/messages
**Auth:** none — session token validated via httpOnly cookie
**Purpose:** Retrieve all chat messages for an anonymous session (for page reload recovery).

**Request:** none

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "messages": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "role": "enum — user | assistant",
      "message_type": "enum — chat | warning | nudge | error",
      "content": "string",
      "created_at": "ISO8601 timestamp",
      "sequence": "integer"
    }
  ],
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "expires_at": "ISO8601 timestamp"
}
```

**Errors:**
- `404` — session not found
- `410` — session expired

---

### POST /v1/auth/sso
**Auth:** none
**Purpose:** Complete SSO authentication. Accepts provider token, creates or retrieves user record, claims anonymous session if present, returns auth tokens.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "provider": "enum — google | apple",
  "id_token": "string — provider-issued ID token",
  "anonymous_session_id": "uuid | null — present if logged-out session exists to carry over"
}
```

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "access_token": "string — short-lived JWT",
  "refresh_token": "string — secure rotation token (httpOnly cookie)",
  "user": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "email": "string",
    "display_name": "string | null",
    "is_new_user": "boolean — true if account was just created"
  },
  "conversation_carry_over": {
    "success": "boolean",
    "message_count": "integer — number of messages carried over (0 if none or failed)",
    "conversation_id": "uuid | null — authenticated conversation ID if carry-over succeeded",
    "summary_generated": "boolean — true if ConversationSummary was generated from carried-over messages"
  }
}
```

**Claim operation sequence (atomic):**
1. Validate SSO provider token
2. Create or retrieve User record
3. If anonymous_session_id present and session is active: promote eligible messages (message_type = 'chat') from session_id lineage to authenticated Conversation entity — re-key from session_id to user_id + conversation_id
4. Trigger ConversationSummary generation synchronously from promoted messages
5. Return response only after steps 3 and 4 complete (or fail)

**Summary generation failure handling:** If ConversationSummary generation fails, the claim is still considered successful (messages are promoted, conversation_carry_over.success = true, summary_generated = false). On the first authenticated message, the Orchestrator context reconstruction will load promoted raw messages for recency context but will have no synthesized summary — coaching context is partially impaired for this session only. This is an acceptable degraded state.

**Errors:**
- `400` — invalid or expired provider token
- `401` — SSO provider authentication failed
- `409` — SSO subject already linked to a different account (edge case for future multi-provider support)
- `500` — carry-over failed (auth still succeeds; conversation_carry_over.success = false in response)

### POST /v1/auth/refresh
**Auth:** refresh token (httpOnly cookie)
**Purpose:** Rotate access token using refresh token.

**Request:**
```json
{}
```

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "access_token": "string — new short-lived JWT"
}
```

**Errors:**
- `401` — refresh token invalid or expired; client must re-authenticate

---

**Auth:** required — Bearer token; any authenticated user (role: authenticated_user) holding a valid Bearer access token may call this endpoint
**Purpose:** Invalidate current session tokens.

```

{}
```

**Auth:** required — Bearer token; any authenticated user (role: authenticated_user) holding a valid Bearer access token may call this endpoint, subject to the 403 ownership check
**Purpose:** Retrieve messages for an authenticated conversation (used on home screen load after carry-over).

      "content": "string",
      "created_at": "ISO8601 timestamp",
      "sequence": "integer"

## State Management

**Global auth state** is held in a top-level context provider (`AuthContext`). Fields: `authState` ('loading' | 'authenticated' | 'unauthenticated' | 'token-expired'), `user` (null or User object), `accessToken` (in memory only — never persisted to localStorage).

**Anonymous session state** is held in `SessionContext`. Fields: `sessionId` (from httpOnly cookie, mirrored in memory), `expiresAt`, `ttlSeconds`, `warningFired` (boolean — tracks whether the 10-minute warning has fired this session to prevent re-firing). The `warningFired` flag is reset only on explicit sign-out or new session creation — never on inactivity timer reset within the same session.

**Conversation state** is held in `ConversationContext`. Fields: `messages` (ordered array), `isLoading`, `nudgeFired` (boolean — tracks whether the in-conversation nudge has fired this logged-out session). On authentication, `messages` is replaced with the carried-over conversation from the API response.

**Inactivity timer** runs client-side in memory. Resets on: message send, keystroke in prompt bar, any button tap. When `ttlSeconds` drops to 600 (10 minutes) and `warningFired` is false and message count > 0, the session expiry warning banner renders and `warningFired` is set to true. Timer is not persisted — on page reload, TTL is recalculated from `expires_at` returned by the session API.

**Navigate-away protection** uses `beforeunload` and `popstate` event listeners. Guards fire only when: `authState === 'unauthenticated'` AND `messages.length > 0`. Modal state (open/closed) is local to the guard component.

**Auth state resolution on page load:** UI defaults to `authState: 'loading'` immediately. Silent token validation runs in background. On success: transition to 'authenticated'. On failure after 1 attempt with exponential backoff (max 3s total): transition to 'unauthenticated' and render auth-resolution-failed banner. HTTP 401 or 403 on token validation = confirmed auth failure.

## Integration Points

**Google Sign-In (OAuth 2.0 / OIDC)**
- What: ID token exchange for user identity
- Why: SSO provider per AC#5, AC#26
- Failure mode: provider unavailable → Auth Sheet error state; invalid token → 400 from /v1/auth/sso; retry unlimited times

**Apple Sign-In (OAuth 2.0 / OIDC)**
- What: ID token exchange for user identity
- Why: SSO provider per AC#5, AC#26
- Failure mode: identical to Google Sign-In failure handling

**AI Service (Orchestrator)**
- What: Streaming conversational AI responses for both logged-out and authenticated users
- Why: Core product functionality
- Orchestrator context for anonymous sessions: message content only — no user profile, no conversation summaries, no connected device data, no sub-agent dispatch for integration data or memory retrieval. The Orchestrator runs the LLM reasoning step and returns a generic, non-personalized streaming response. This is the same streaming infrastructure as authenticated sessions with a reduced context payload, not a separate pipeline.
- Orchestrator context for authenticated sessions: full context — user profile, recent conversation summaries, connected device data, full sub-agent dispatch per Section 2 of system architecture.
- Failure mode: 503 from message endpoint → AI-unavailable error banner; retry triggered by next user message send; no automatic retry

**Session TTL Enforcement (Backend)**
- What: Server-side expiry of anonymous sessions at 60-minute inactivity threshold
- Why: Per AC#23; server is authoritative for session state
- Failure mode: Client-side timer drift is acceptable — server 410 response is the authoritative expiry signal; client transitions to expired state on 410

## Non-Functional Requirements

- **Latency:** Auth SSO endpoint P99 < 2s (excluding SSO provider round-trip). Anonymous message endpoint time-to-first-token P99 < 1.5s. Session activity reset P99 < 200ms. "Not signed in" indicator reappears within 500ms of access token expiry detection on the client (AC#27).
- **Caching:** Auth tokens cached in memory only (never localStorage). Anonymous session TTL cached in SessionContext, refreshed on activity reset response. No caching of AI responses.
- **Rate limiting:** Anonymous session creation: 10 per IP per hour. Anonymous message send: 30 per session. Auth SSO: 10 attempts per IP per 15 minutes. All enforced at API gateway layer.
- **Error handling:** All API errors return `{ "error": { "code": "string", "message": "string" } }` envelope. Client maps error codes to UI states. 5xx errors always surface user-friendly inline messages — raw error details never exposed to client.
- **Session storage:** Anonymous session ID mirrored in memory from httpOnly cookie. No conversation data in localStorage or sessionStorage — all logged-out conversation data lives server-side, keyed to the anonymous session.
- **Token security:** Access tokens held in memory only. Refresh tokens in httpOnly cookies. No tokens in localStorage or sessionStorage.
- **Reduced motion:** All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. Glow effect static at 75% opacity. All fade/scale animations replaced with instant state transitions.
- **Inactivity timer reset:** Each user action (message send, keystroke, tap) resets the inactivity timer server-side within one request-response cycle, with a maximum server-side propagation delay of 500ms (AC#23).

## System Architecture Updates Required

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md — Section 13: Data Model Overview]

**AnonymousSession** — ephemeral session record for unauthenticated users. Keyed by UUID, set as httpOnly cookie on first message send. TTL: 60 minutes of inactivity, enforced server-side. Status lifecycle: active → expired | claimed. Claimed on successful SSO authentication; session messages re-keyed to authenticated user's Conversation entity atomically.

**ConversationMessage** — individual message record supporting both anonymous and authenticated sessions. Dual-keyed: references either `session_id` (anonymous) or `user_id` + `conversation_id` (authenticated). `message_type` field (chat | warning | nudge | error) controls carry-over eligibility — only 'chat' type messages are promoted to the authenticated conversation on session claim. `role` field (user | assistant | system) aligns with LLM turn structure.

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md — Section 2: Agentic Orchestration Layer — 2.4 Anonymous Session Handling]

**Anonymous session requests:** The Orchestrator handles anonymous (unauthenticated) session requests using the same streaming infrastructure as authenticated requests but with a reduced context payload. For anonymous sessions, the Orchestrator receives only the message content — no user profile, no conversation summaries, no connected device data. No sub-agents are dispatched for integration data retrieval, profile reads, or memory retrieval. The Orchestrator runs the LLM reasoning step and returns a generic, non-personalized streaming response. This ensures feature parity on the streaming delivery path while enforcing the product constraint that logged-out users receive non-personalized responses (AC#28). No separate pipeline is maintained for anonymous sessions.

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md — Section 4.2: Conversation History]

**Anonymous session data lineage:** Anonymous session messages are a separate data lineage from authenticated conversation transcripts. They are stored server-side in the `conversation_messages` table keyed to `session_id` (not `conversation_id`) and are not accessible through the authenticated conversation history until a session claim occurs. On successful SSO authentication, eligible messages (message_type = 'chat') are atomically promoted from the anonymous session lineage into the authenticated user's Conversation entity — re-keyed from `session_id` to `user_id` + `conversation_id`. This promotion is the only path from anonymous to authenticated data lineage; no merge of anonymous messages into an existing authenticated conversation transcript is permitted.

**ConversationSummary generation after carry-over:** ConversationSummary generation is triggered synchronously as part of the anonymous session claim operation, immediately after message promotion completes. The `/v1/auth/sso` response is not returned until summary generation completes or fails. If summary generation fails, the claim is still considered successful (messages are promoted) and `summary_generated: false` is returned in the response. On the first authenticated message after a failed summary generation, the Orchestrator loads promoted raw messages for recency context but has no synthesized summary — coaching context is partially impaired for that session only. ConversationSummary generation is never triggered for anonymous sessions that expire without authentication.

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md — Section 4.3: Context Reconstruction]

**First authenticated session after anonymous session carry-over:** When a user's first authenticated session follows an anonymous session carry-over, context reconstruction at session start proceeds as follows: (1) load structured user state (may be empty for new users); (2) load ConversationSummary generated from carried-over messages if summary generation succeeded; (3) if summary generation failed during claim, load promoted raw messages directly as recency context (capped at last 20 messages to control context window size). This ensures the Orchestrator has meaningful context on the first authenticated message even in the degraded summary-generation-failed case.

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md — Section 7.3: Authentication]

**Anonymous session management:** Unauthenticated users receive an anonymous session UUID on first message send, delivered as an httpOnly cookie (SameSite=Lax, Secure flag required). Session TTL is 60 minutes of inactivity, enforced server-side. On SSO authentication, the anonymous session is claimed atomically — all eligible messages (message_type = 'chat') are re-keyed from session_id to the authenticated user's conversation record, and a ConversationSummary is generated synchronously from the promoted messages. The claim operation is atomic; partial claims are not permitted. If claim fails, authentication still succeeds and the client renders a carry-over-failed inline message.

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md — Section 20: User Tier Architecture — 20.1 Unauthenticated Free Tier]

Unauthenticated users interact via anonymous sessions (see Section 13). Logged-out AI responses are generic and non-personalized — no goals, no profile context, no prior session memory. The Orchestrator receives no user context for anonymous sessions and dispatches no sub-agents for integration data, profile reads, or memory retrieval (see Section 2.4). Anonymous session data is never accessible across session boundaries and is purged server-side on TTL expiry. Anonymous session messages exist in a separate data lineage from authenticated conversation transcripts and are promoted to the authenticated lineage only on successful session claim (see Section 4.2).

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]
